enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about his first day riding his motorbike, describing struggles with gear direction, front-brake habits from riding bicycles, and the general intimidation of the bike's power. He also briefly mentions a confusing bank loan interest situation and plans to organize his website with workout video content.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Excited but genuinely intimidated by the power; committed to practicing all day until it feels natural
Frustrated and confused that his monthly payment is less than his monthly interest, wants answers
Wants to collect videos of strong women lifting as motivation and cross-reference material

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday, Kyle worked on his motorbike and had to remove the rack and tool bag to put the side panel on correctly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle took his motorbike out for the first time yesterday but had the gears in reverse, causing it to stall repeatedly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle initially thought pushing the gear lever down meant a higher gear and pulling it up meant a lower gear, but he realized he was wrong.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle felt freaked out by the motorbike's power and worried about accidentally doing a wheelie due to a twitch.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to drive around North Boulder on dirt roads today to get more comfortable with the motorbike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has a habit of only using the front brake and slamming on it because he's not used to disc brakes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle often forgets to use the turn signal or turn it off while riding the motorbike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle was a little high while riding the motorbike but doesn't think it affected his performance.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle owns a Kawasaki KLR 400, which he describes as a 'daddy bike' capable of going from zero to 60 in under five seconds.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle noticed that his bank is charging him $50 a month in interest, but the bank website shows his monthly payment is only $31.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to call his bank to clarify the interest rate discrepancy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle organized his files and folders on his computer yesterday but didn't work on his website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle wants to create videos of strong, attractive women working out to inspire himself.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle feels weak and unable to lift heavy weights like the women he admires.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to focus on riding his motorbike today and hopes he doesn't crash.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is unsure if his Suzuki DRZ 400 motorbike has a gas gauge and doesn't know how to check the gas level.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is also unsure where to put the brake fluid in his motorbike.

transcript.text: What's up? Living in Boulder Vlog for the 15th of May, 2014.
Yesterday was, I think, Wednesday.
So yesterday, I did two big places, although the memory of it is kind of fazey.
I don't know, I did one place. The two big places was yesterday.
So I came back early, like noon, and, oh yeah, I proceeded to work on my motorbike.
I had to put the side panel on, but in order to put the right side panel on,
I had to freaking take my rack and tool bag and everything off, dude.
And then I put the side panel on, but I put it on wrong,
So I had to take everything off to put it back on right,
Because I had to stick it from the front through some hole.
But then I, oh, I took my motorbike out for the first time yesterday, dude.
But I had the gears wrong. I had the gears in reverse.
I thought that pushing it down meant that that's a higher gear,
And pulling it up means a lower gear.
So I was like, it wasn't working. It'd go down once, and I'd be like,
Wait, is it broken? Is it broken?
And then I'd gear up, and it'd be like, why is it gearing so many?
So I was trying to start it in fifth gear, and it kept stalling,
And I'm like, oh, no, this is so hard. I didn't know that.
But I think I figured it out. Then I figured it out.
And I think I was doing pretty good by then,
But I'd kind of lost my taste for it.
It's kind of freaky, dude, because that thing has as much power as you want it to have.
I was like, whoa, holy shit.
Like, I feel like if I accidentally, like, slip my hand, like I have a twitch.
Sometimes I have twitches. Like, what happens if you actually have a twitch?
Let's do a fucking wheelie and wipe out.
That's what I feel like anyway.
But today I'm going to, when I get back from work,
I'm going to drive up into North Boulder around all the dirt roads
And just drive literally all freaking day until it becomes second nature to me.
I also have a problem with, like, only using the front brake and slamming on it
Because I'm not used to disc brakes.
I'm used to, like, crappy brakes.
So I kept, like, slamming on the front brake and I'm doing endos and shit.
And then what else?
Oh, yeah, I forget to do the turn signal,
And when I do the turn signal, I forget to turn it off.
It's like all this shit you have to remember.
Yeah, and I was a little bit high, but I don't think that hurt anything
Because I'm desensitized.
But, yeah, today I'll do that.
I'll get it down.
I'll get it down pat.
Because I have a, what is it, a Kawasaki KLX 400.
So those things can go from 0 to 16 in, like, under 5 seconds, I think.
Quarter mile in, like, 13 seconds.
And this has, like, knobbies on it.
So, yeah, dude.
It's a daddy bike.
I want to see what it can do out there on those dirt roads.
So, yeah, but now, the guy said I'm spending, at the bank said I'm spending, like, 50 bucks a month just on interest.
But then I looked on the bank website, and it said that my monthly payment is 31.
So how can my monthly payment be less on my interest?
So I'm going to have to call him up and be like, what the fuck?
What's the real interest?
Anyways.
It's 19.5, and I owe, like, 3,700 now.
God damn.
Anyways.
What else?
I didn't do any work on my website yesterday.
Oh, but I dragged and dropped.
I organized my cell phone a little bit more with new folders and videos and stuff.
I want to get a lot of cool videos on my cell phone of chicks working out, doing strong shit.
It's all the strong hot chicks.
Because then I can go, like, CrossFit chase and stuff.
Because then I can go into the gym, and I can, like, watch the girl, like, dumbbell press, 100-pound dumbbells, and be like, okay, cool, let's give it a shot.
Of course, I can't do that right now.
So I'm like, oh, my God.
I'm so weak.
But, yeah, but I didn't finish.
I need to finish that today.
And then, yes, I guess that's it.
I guess that's it.
I'd like to, yeah, so today's going to be all about riding the motorcycle.
So wish me luck.
Hopefully I don't do an endo or whatever.
Hey, does anybody know motorbikes?
Anybody familiar with my motorbike, which is otherwise known as a Suzuki DRZ400?
Like, is there a gas gauge on it?
Like, where the fuck is the gas?
How do you know how much gas is left in it?
And where do you put the brake fluid?
Okay, so that's that.
Peace out.
